title: `WHERE field` does not use PK or data skipping indices
description:
### Use case
A query like `SELECT * FROM t WHERE id` will not use existing PK or data skipping indices on the `id` column. The equivalent query `SELECT * FROM t WHERE id != 0` will use them.
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/23c280d1-8898-436c-9081-1ae593b14c3d
```sql
CREATE TABLE t (
id UInt16,
INDEX id_index id TYPE minmax
) ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1);
-- Does not use PK/indexes
EXPLAIN indexes = 1 SELECT * FROM t WHERE id;
/*
Expression ((Project names + Projection))
Filter ((WHERE + Change column names to column identifiers))
ReadFromMergeTree (default.t)
Indexes:
PrimaryKey
Condition: true
Parts: 1/1
Granules: 1/1
Ranges: 1
*/
-- Uses PK/indexes
EXPLAIN indexes = 1 SELECT * FROM t WHERE id != 0;
/*
Expression ((Project names + Projection))
Filter ((WHERE + Change column names to column identifiers))
ReadFromMergeTree (default.t)
Indexes:
PrimaryKey
Keys:
id
Condition: (id not in [0, 0])
Parts: 1/1
Granules: 1/1
Search Algorithm: generic exclusion search
Skip
Name: id_index
Description: minmax GRANULARITY 1
Parts: 1/1
Granules: 1/1
Ranges: 1
*/
```
This is particularly noticeable when accessing boolean fields, as it is common to reference them by name like `WHERE field` and `WHERE NOT field`.
It would be nice to make those constructs able to use indices.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id UInt16, value VARCHAR(255), INDEX value_index(value) USING BTREE);
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE t2 (id UInt16, is_active UInt8, INDEX active_index(is_active) USING BTREE);
key_bug_features: WHERE clause does not utilize primary key or indices
